key_insight: The paper derives exact worst- and best-case distorted utility values under moment ambiguity by working in the quantile domain and using nonsmooth variational analysis plus isotonic projection.
engineering_relevance: This provides a drop-in inner oracle for distributionally robust optimization in pricing, inventory, contracting, and capacity planning, especially when only moments are known.
method: Reformulate the moment-based worst-case distorted-utility problem in quantile space.
Apply exact first-order optimality conditions for locally Lipschitz, possibly nonsmooth utilities and general distortions.
Use isotonic projection onto the monotone cone to efficiently enforce quantile monotonicity with linear scaling in discretization.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports closed-form extremal values and distributions, and demonstrates the method on three classical extensions plus a real capacity-provisioning case for generative AI inference.
watch_item: The approach depends on accurate moment specifications and careful numerical handling of the monotone-cone projection when the utility or distortion is highly nonsmooth.
paper_title: Extreme-Case Distorted Utility under Moment Ambiguity
